candidates.row_label: Depreciation/Amortization in Cost of goods sold
candidates.why: Explicitly labelled as depreciation/amortization within the selected parent row 'Cost of goods sold', so it appears to be a sub-component already embedded in the selected COGS bucket rather than a separate same-level expense to add.
This row is also labelled as being within COR/COGS and appears to represent a COGS sub-component, not a separate operating line to add; it is also unpopulated in the excerpt.
Also phrased as a within-COGS component and unpopulated in the excerpt, so there is no clear separate D&A line to add beyond the selected COGS row.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is '[Income Statement] Cost of goods sold'. The visible COGS D&A rows are labelled as being in/within COGS, which indicates they are components of the selected parent row rather than separate same-level rows to add later. Conservative no-double-counting treatment is to add nothing separately.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation and amortization of selling expenses
candidates.why: Explicitly labelled as depreciation/amortization of the selected parent row 'Selling expenses', so it appears to be embedded within that selected XSGA component rather than a separate expense to add.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation/Amortization in General and administrative expenses
candidates.why: Explicitly labelled as being within the selected parent row 'General and administrative expenses', indicating a sub-component already included in the selected XSGA bucket.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA_COMPONENTS reference bucket is the sum of '[Income Statement] Selling expenses' and '[Income Statement] General and administrative expenses'. The visible D&A rows are specifically labelled as within those parent rows, so adding them separately would risk double counting. No separate above-operating-profit R&D D&A row is visible.
